key_insight: MaterEval turns expert materials rules into preference signals by contrasting an informed judgment with a blind guess, teaching LLMs to make evidence-backed evaluations instead of intuition-only predictions.
engineering_relevance: This provides a low-cost screening-and-review module for autonomous materials discovery pipelines, especially when large candidate sets make manual evaluation impractical.
method: Generate two evaluations for the same candidate: rule-following informed judgment and rule-removed blind guess.
Use the paired outputs as preference data to steer general-purpose LLMs toward reliable, evidence-supported conclusions.
Apply a fast-slow reasoning scheme that screens many candidates quickly and reserves deeper review for a smaller subset.
evidence_signal: In high-entropy alloy assessment, small open-source LLMs improved accuracy, conclusion consistency, and evidence discrimination without external retrieval, approaching rule-based closed-source models.
watch_item: Performance depends on how well expert rules are encoded into the preference signal, so rule coverage and domain transferability may limit deployment quality.
paper_title: From Blind Guess to Informed Judgment: Teaching LLMs to Evaluate Materials by Building Knowledge-Augmented Preference Signals
